signal_advisor.hook_title: OJK Cybersecurity Mandate + Engineering Scale-Up: FLOQ Building a Regulated Crypto Exchange Without Code-Level Security Controls
signal_advisor.hook_rationale: FLOQ is a licensed OJK crypto exchange actively building its engineering organisation from scratch (hiring across Backend, DevOps, iOS, Android, Golang, and QA) while simultaneously facing binding OJK cybersecurity guidelines (August 2025) that mandate Zero Trust, ISO/NIST-aligned cyber risk management, and secure systems — all tied to their POJK 27/2024 operating licence. The combination of a rapid engineering build-out and a live regulatory compliance obligation creates genuine tension: code is being written at pace by a growing team with no visible AppSec controls, directly under a regulator that can revoke their licence. This is not a generic fintech risk story — it is a specific, time-pressured gap between what OJK requires and what FLOQ currently has.
signal_advisor.strategy_note: Lead with the OJK Cybersecurity Guideline for DFA Trading Organizers (August 2025) as the primary hook — this is a binding, sector-specific mandate that directly names FLOQ's licence category and requires implementation of Zero Trust principles and cyber risk management aligned to ISO, NIST, and BSSN frameworks. Layer in the hiring signal as the tension multiplier: FLOQ is simultaneously scaling engineers across Backend (Golang/microservices), DevOps, iOS, Android, and QA on AWS infrastructure built from scratch since 2025 — meaning new code is being written at velocity by a growing team with no publicly visible SAST or AppSec tooling in the pipeline. The core message is: FLOQ is building a regulated exchange under active OJK cybersecurity scrutiny, and every line of code being merged today is a compliance liability if there are no automated security quality gates in place. Sonar addresses this directly — SonarQube or SonarCloud integrates into their CI/CD pipeline to enforce secure-by-design at the code level, catches vulnerabilities (OWASP, CWE) before they reach production, and generates the audit artefacts OJK's cybersecurity framework demands. Target Felix Tan (Engineering Director, Red/Blue) with urgency and consequence framing: the OJK guideline is live, their team is growing, and the window to embed security controls before the codebase scales further is closing. Target Yudhono Rawis (Engineering Director, Blue/Green) with process and framework alignment: Sonar maps directly to ISO 27001, NIST, and BSSN frameworks cited in OJK's own guideline.

suggested_signals.label: OJK Cybersecurity Guideline for DFA Operators — Aug 2025
suggested_signals.content: In August 2025, OJK launched a binding Cybersecurity Guideline specifically for Digital Financial Assets (DFA) Trading Organizers in Indonesia — the exact licence category FLOQ holds under POJK 27/2024. The guideline mandates: implementation of Zero Trust principles; cyber risk management aligned to ISO, NIST, CSMA, BSSN, and CREAST frameworks; secure systems; data protection; and audit readiness. FLOQ is confirmed on OJK's official whitelist of licensed DFA traders (published via Tempo.co), meaning this guideline applies to them directly. Non-compliance puts their operating licence at risk. There is no public evidence that FLOQ has deployed any SAST, DevSecOps, or code-level security tooling to meet these requirements.
suggested_signals.why: A live, sector-specific OJK cybersecurity mandate with direct licence implications gives Sonar an immediate, compliance-backed reason to reach out — this is not hypothetical risk, it is a regulatory obligation FLOQ must demonstrate it is meeting.

suggested_signals.label: Engineering scale-up: 8+ roles, Golang/microservices/AWS, no AppSec
suggested_signals.content: As of June 2026, FLOQ is actively hiring across DevOps Engineer, multiple Backend Engineers, iOS Engineer, Lead Front End Engineer (Next.js/React), Android Developer, Golang Engineer, QA Engineer, Senior Architect, and Data Engineer (AWS-required) — all listed on Kalibrr under FLOQ's company profile. LinkedIn evidence confirms the engineering stack includes microservices, AWS infrastructure, and an ISO27001-compliant architecture being built from scratch since 2025. No AppSec, DevSecOps, or security engineering roles are among the open positions, indicating security is not being hired for as a dedicated function. This means code quality and security responsibility falls on generalist engineers building at pace.
suggested_signals.why: A fast-growing engineering team building a regulated fintech platform from scratch, with no dedicated AppSec function and no visible security tooling, is precisely the environment where Sonar's automated code quality gates and vulnerability detection deliver immediate, measurable risk reduction.
